assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a text editor that can open a local HTML file.
No Ably account, app, or API key exists yet.

developer_goal: Create an Ably account and app, then complete the official JavaScript getting-started guide through the first realtime connection.

first_success.outcome: A local HTML page connects to Ably Realtime with the JavaScript SDK.
first_success.observable_signal: The page and browser console display Made my first connection!

route_selection.surface: Ably signup plus Pub/Sub JavaScript getting-started guide
route_selection.rule: Use the official browser JavaScript CDN guide after account and API key creation; leave unverified while post-signup onboarding states remain undocumented.

candidates.name: Email signup, create app and API key, local HTML CDN connection
candidates.reason: This matches the documented JavaScript getting-started terminal, but post-signup verification or onboarding screens are not enumerated.

candidates.name: GitHub or Google signup then same JavaScript guide
candidates.reason: The public signup page offers identity-provider continues that may change account-step fields versus email registration.

candidates.name: Node.js getting-started guide
candidates.reason: It reaches a similar connection log line but adds Node project setup beyond the browser HTML route.

required_path.action: Create an Ably account with the email signup route.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Cookies Policy agreement

required_path.observable_result: Signup is submitted; the next authenticated dashboard or verification state is not fully public.

required_path.action: Create a new app in the Ably dashboard and create an API key with publish, subscribe, presence, and history capabilities.
required_path.observable_result: An API key string is available for the HTML sample.

required_path.action: Create index.html with the guide's CDN SDK script, API key, clientId, and connected-event handler.
required_path.observable_result: The local HTML file is ready to open in a browser.

required_path.action: Open index.html in a browser and confirm the first connection message.
required_path.observable_result: The page logs Made my first connection!

prerequisites.description: The getting-started guide uses an API key for simplicity and expects publish, subscribe, presence, and history capabilities.

platform_outcomes.description: Ably establishes the realtime connection when the SDK reaches the connected state.

excluded.item: Publishing, presence, history, CLI install, and production token authentication
excluded.reason: These occur after the guide's first connection signal.

excluded.item: Optional product-updates marketing checkbox on signup
excluded.reason: The public signup page presents product updates as optional.

sources.title: Getting started: Pub/Sub in JavaScript
evidence_supported: Create app and API key capabilities

sources.title: Authentication overview
evidence_supported: Dashboard API key creation and capabilities

sources.title: Account overview
evidence_supported: Account and app platform context after signup

uncertainties.question: What verification, confirmation, or onboarding states follow Sign up before the first Ably dashboard session?
uncertainties.impact: The mandatory account segment after form submit cannot be reconstructed or counted from the public signup page alone.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough from ably.com/sign-up submit through the first authenticated dashboard app list.

uncertainties.question: Does a brand-new Ably account already contain a default app and API key, or must the developer create both before the JavaScript sample works?
uncertainties.impact: App and key creation may be automatic platform outcomes or required developer actions.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official post-signup dashboard state for a new free account, or a fresh-account observation of default versus empty apps.
